result.notes: Guard order enforced in decideDispatch (gate->suppression->frequency-cap->quiet-hours->send) lazy short-circuit + stoppedAt; proven via aws-sdk-client-mock SendEmail counts (0 on refuse/skip/defer, 1 on send, 1 on replay). Atomic claim buildOutboxClaim (UPDATE WHERE status=pending RETURNING); dedupe-idempotency integration proves concurrent/replayed send exactly once. messages_log+usage_counters(emails_sent)+outbox mark-sent in ONE workspace-scoped tx (asserts every stmt binds workspace_id $1); forced-failure rollback verified. workspace_id from outbox row never SQS body. Suppression per-(workspace_id,email) OR global_hard_bounces, citext; A doesn't block B. sendEmail wrapper over SESv2 SendEmailCommand. From=no-reply@<from_domain>; quiet/cap config from outbox.payload; non-send outcomes reset(defer)/terminal(skip/refuse); handler bounds retries MAX_ATTEMPTS=5 -> DLQ. Scope: dispatch only, no feedback/unsubscribe.
result.testSummary: Dispatcher 52 tests (28 unit + 14 integration on real PG localhost:5433); email +1 sendEmail (now 27). Full suite TWICE from cold both green, integration ran, phases 2-6 unchanged (db 13, segments 61, tenancy 41, ingest 22, processor 50+1 skip, onboarding 38, authorizer 18, api 17, batch-eval 11, tests 7). Only skip pre-existing processor LocalStack e2e.
